Package algorithm.gaaco

Class LocalSearch

java.lang.Object
algorithm.gaaco.LocalSearch
public final class LocalSearch extends Object
Stateless local-search passes for TSP-style open-path optimisation.

All methods are pure functions: they accept a Route and return a (possibly improved) Route. The input route is never modified. Both passes are called as the final "phase 3" of the GA→ACO→LocalSearch fusion pipeline in GaAcoPlanner, and the 2-opt pass is also applied to the GA best route before it seeds the ACO phase.

Open-path semantics

The UAV collects data in a single sweep and does not return to its origin, so the path is treated as an open Hamiltonian path (not a TSP cycle). Edge costs involve only consecutive pairs in the visit order; there is no return edge from the last node back to the first.

Typical usage

   Route r = aco.refine(gaRoute, nodes);
   r = LocalSearch.twoOpt(r);    // eliminate crossings
   r = LocalSearch.orOpt(r);     // relocate stray waypoints

    static Route
    orOpt(Route route)
    Or-opt local search: relocates single nodes to cheaper positions in the path until no further improvement is possible.
    static Route

    2-opt local search: iteratively reverses path segments to eliminate crossing edges until no further improvement is possible.

  • Method Details

    • twoOpt

      public static Route twoOpt(Route route)
      2-opt local search: iteratively reverses path segments to eliminate crossing edges until no further improvement is possible.

      Move semantics (open path)

      For each pair (i, j) with i < j, reversing the segment [i+1 … j] changes two "connecting" edges:
      • Removed: (i → i+1) and (j → j+1) if j < n-1
      • Added: (i → j) and (i+1 → j+1) if j < n-1
      When j = n-1 only the single edge (i → i+1) is removed and replaced by (i → n-1); the internal segment distances cancel symmetrically because distances are Euclidean (symmetric).

      The move is applied whenever total added distance is less than total removed distance by more than a numerical tolerance (1e-10).

      Complexity: O(n²) per pass; converges in at most O(n²) passes. For the node counts typical in this simulation (≤ 50) this is near-instant.

      Parameters:
      route - route to improve; must not be null
      Returns:
      improved route (may be identical to input if already locally optimal)

    • orOpt

      public static Route orOpt(Route route)
      Or-opt local search: relocates single nodes to cheaper positions in the path until no further improvement is possible.

      Move semantics

      For each node at position i, the algorithm computes the cost saved by removing it from its current position (the "gap" is closed with a direct edge between its neighbours), then evaluates the cost of reinserting it at every other position in the path. The best net improvement across all candidate positions is applied if positive, then the search restarts from the beginning of the (now-modified) path.

      Complementary to 2-opt

      While 2-opt eliminates crossing edges by reversing segments, Or-opt moves individual waypoints. The two passes address different structural defects and are strongest when applied in sequence: 2-opt first, then Or-opt.

      Open-path boundary handling

      The first and last nodes have only one neighbouring edge. The removal saving for a path-end node is simply the cost of that single edge (removing the endpoint shortens the path by that amount with no repair edge needed).

      Complexity: O(n²) per pass. For the small node counts used in this simulation (≤ 50) this is negligible.

      Parameters:
      route - route to improve; must not be null
      Returns:
      improved route (may be identical to input if already locally optimal)
